name: Akpith (Corn/maize & Bean Flour Snack)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, bring water to a boil.
2. Gradually add half of the corn/maize meal and all of the soy flour to the boiling water, stirring constantly until a thick porridge forms.
3. Add baking powder, chili powder (if using), and sugar; season to taste.
4. Cook over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring continuously.
5. Remove from heat and let cool for about 10 minutes.
6. Mix in the remaining corn/maize meal and water to achieve a stiff dough.
7. Heat oil in a pan or wok to a depth of approximately 2 inches (5 cm).
8. Form the dough into balls, each about 1 inch (2.5 cm) in diameter.
9. Carefully add 4-5 balls to the hot oil and cook for 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
(Note: The history and cultural significance of maize are noted but not part of the recipe instructions.)
